title: quercus gracilenta
intro: Quercus gracilenta, also known as the Coast Live Oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its broad canopy and twisted branches. The bark of the Quercus gracilenta contains high levels of tannins and flavonoids, which possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus gracilenta include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Quercus gracilenta include tinctures, infusions, and teas, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
